sections.question: Whether a citizens' group conducting a petition against waste incineration needs to file campaign disclosure forms, and whether money raised from fundraising activities (yard sales, painting services) falls under reporting requirements when a committee has not yet been formally formed.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a nascent citizens' petition group must file campaign finance disclosures and whether funds raised from informal fundraising activities are subject to reporting requirements before a formal committee is established.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This appears to be an incoming request letter rather than an FPPC advice response. The document is poorly written and difficult to parse; references to 'farm 410' and other details are unclear. Date discrepancy: letter dated Feb 23, 1990 but references 'last Friday in June 1990'. Handwritten portions are largely illegible. No formal question and conclusion sections present. Low confidence due to document quality and unclear legal issues being raised.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a citizens' group conducting a petition against waste incineration needs to file campaign disclosure forms, and whether money raised from fundraising activities (yard sales, painting services) falls under reporting requirements when a committee has not yet been formally formed.
embedding.first_500_words: Mr. Alice Atwood Feb 23, 1990 This is to confirm our conversation of the last Friday in June 1990. A group of citizens here in Monroe are in the process of conducting a petition against "waste incineration." We haven't formed a committee so if you don't do or would not turn in the petition to me if we attain enough valid signatures. If we have valid signatures then we must petition the City Council to act as a check for our action. At this point we will see all likelihood form a committee. You had said a city farm wouldn't have to be filled out with me because a committee & the City Council act as that for whatever of all agricultural use held. The money we have raised to date is from Paint Cottage & the best part on painting picture, a yard sale, etc. Our attorney informed me that the money raised to date should fall under the farm 410 as we and a committee & the petition are still being circulated. Please reserve my duty in confirming our conversation but give them only till. Your written approval & confirmation would be appreciated. Sincerely, John Anthony 4155 E. Rac Ph = 209-251-1346 The handwritten text on this page is extremely faint and mostly illegible, with only scattered fragments of words and numbers partially visible. Here's what can be reliably transcribed: "8-30-68" There are several handwritten notes or numbers that appear to be dates or references, such as "8-30-68," but the majority of the content cannot be reliably transcribed due to the faintness and quality of the handwriting.
embedding.summary: An incoming request from a Monroe citizen asking for written confirmation and approval regarding disclosure filing obligations for a citizens' petition campaign against waste incineration and the treatment of funds raised through informal fundraising activities.
